title: DNS Explained
summary: Every server has a number address called an IP address.
Names like example.com are easy for people, numbers are easy for computers.
DNS turns the name you type into the matching number.
It works like a phone book for the internet.

sections.text: You type a name like example.com, but computers do not find each other by name. They use numbers. Something quietly translates between the two every time, and it happens so fast you never notice.

sections.title: Why Names and Numbers?
sections.text: Every server on the internet has an address made of numbers, called an IP address. Numbers are easy for computers but hard for people. Names like example.com are easy for people. We need a way to connect the two.

sections.title: What DNS Does
sections.text: DNS stands for Domain Name System. When you type a website name, DNS looks up the matching number and hands it back, so your browser knows which server to talk to.

media.alt: A name being looked up and matched to a number

sections.title: A Phone Book for the Internet
sections.text: DNS is like the contacts app on your phone. You remember a person's name, not their phone number. You tap the name, and your phone finds the number for you. DNS does the same thing for websites.

media.alt: Tapping a contact name and the phone finding the number

sections.title: What Happens When You Type an Address
items: You type a website name into the browser.
The browser asks a DNS server which number goes with this name.
The DNS server finds the matching IP address.
It sends the number back to your browser.
Your browser uses that number to reach the right server.

sections.text: Your computer remembers recent lookups for a while, so it does not have to ask again every single time. This is called caching, and it makes browsing faster.

terms.definition: The system that turns a website name into its number.
The number address of a server on the internet.
The readable name of a website, like example.com.
A computer that holds the name to number lookups.
A short term memory of recent answers, used to go faster.

options: It turns a website name into its number
It builds web pages from scratch
questions.explanation: DNS takes the name you type and finds the matching IP address so your browser can reach the right server.

options: The number address of a server
questions.explanation: An IP address is the number that identifies a server on the internet.

questions.text: DNS is most like which everyday thing?
options: A contacts list or phone book
questions.explanation: Like a contacts list, DNS lets you use an easy name while it finds the number for you.
